recentItems.title: Five US passengers from hantavirus-hit ship return to home states - Forth.News
recentItems.summary: Five US passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius, which experienced a hantavirus outbreak in 2026, have returned to their home states. The outbreak has resulted in at least three deaths and multiple cases, with remaining passengers and crew placed under quarantine.

recentItems.summary: Health officials confirmed 12 cases of hantavirus, including 3 deaths, among passengers and crew on the 2026 MV Hondius cruise ship; authorities placed 58 individuals under quarantine and stated that the outbreak originated from contaminated bedding.

recentItems.title: Is Hantavirus a threat to Europe? Climate change and habitat destruction are increasing the risk - AOL.com
recentItems.summary: A 2026 outbreak of hantavirus aboard the cruise ship MV Hondius resulted in 12 confirmed cases, 3 deaths, and 157 passengers and crew placed under quarantine, with health officials stating the virus was likely introduced by infected rodents in a port of call and warning that climate change and habitat destruction are elevating the risk of such outbreaks in Europe.
